title: cotula minuta
intro: Cotula minuta, also known as narrow-leaved cotula or cotula, is a small annual plant with white or yellow flowers, native to Australia and New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Cotula minuta lie in its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and other pathogens. The herbal preparations of Cotula minuta can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
